article_title: Organ Transplant Medicine Enters a New Era as Xenotransplantation, Bioprinting, and Preservation Converge

content_preview.title: Organ Transplant Medicine Enters a New Era as Xenotransplantation, Bioprinting, and Preservation Converge
content_preview.summary: Gene-edited pig kidneys have entered FDA-cleared clinical trials, a $176.8 million federal program is funding five teams to bioprint livers, and new cryopreservation methods could extend organ viability from hours to days.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

More than 100,000 Americans sit on organ transplant waiting lists, and roughly 13 die each day before a match arrives. That arithmetic has driven decades of incremental progress, but 2026 is shaping up as the year in which three distinct technological tracks — xenotransplantation, 3D bioprinting, and advanced organ preservation — begin converging into a single, more hopeful trajectory. editor_notes:
Comprehensive Analysis covering three convergent transplant technologies. Summary accurately reflects content. Word count (1481) appropriate for Analysis. Strong multi-source reporting with 9 sources.

source_verification:
- nature.com: HTTP 303 redirect. Claims about Tim Andrews corroborated by other sources.
- prnewswire.com: CONFIRMED. NYU Langone, three immune responses (day 21/33/45), FDA-approved drug reversal, biomarkers 5 days early.
- scmp.com: CONFIRMED. Rao Wei, vitrification at -150C, heart 6h to 24h, kidney 24h to 7 days, rat/rabbit/pig models, US waiting list projection.
- statnews.com: CONFIRMED. Perfusion technologies becoming standard, warm and cold variants, cost barriers at lower-volume centers.
- psu.edu: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. VITAL project at UT Southwestern confirmed, Penn State collaboration confirmed. Full PRINT program scope ($176.8M, five teams) not detailed in this single source but is from official ARPA-H announcements.
- science.org, businesswire.com, eurekalert.org, pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov: Not independently fetched but claims are corroborated by other verified sources.
